name: Attribute Convert POP
description: The Attribute Convert POP lets you copy a POP [[attribute]] from one "attribute class" to another. That is, attributes can be on a point, a vertex, or a primitive. You can, for example convert a primitive attribute to the points of that primitive. In some cases it is ambiguous, like when a point is shared between multiple primitives or multiple vertices, so the POP picks one value and doesn't complain. When it converts, it can delete the original attribute, and it can error or warning if the attribute it creates already exists. See the [[POP to DAT]] to find all the attributes of a POP. See [[Attribute POP]] to copy or rename an attribute within one attribute class. Note: There currently are no attributes on the POP as a whole.

parameters.name: Convert Operation
parameters.label: Convert Operation
parameters.description: Chooses which attribute class (Point, Vertex, Primitive) for convert from and to.

parameters.name: Input Attributes
parameters.label: Input Attributes
parameters.description: Input attributes to convert to the new attribute class.

parameters.name: New Attribute Name
parameters.label: New Attribute Name
parameters.description: Name of new attribute being created.

parameters.name: Notification if Attribute Exists
parameters.label: Notification if Attribute Exists
parameters.description: Whether to display a notification if the attribute already exists on the input.

parameters.name: Override if Attribute Exists
parameters.label: Override if Attribute Exists
parameters.description: When on, overrides an input attribute if a new attribute created by this POP has the same name.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Attribute_Convert_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
